title: Business Model Canvas — Elevator Guide Rails (NAICS 333921)

sections.title: Business Model Archetype
sections.content: **Archetype: specification_critical** — The customer buys to a technical specification (ISO 7465, EN 81-1). The business model must demonstrate conformance, not persuade on features. Value is created through process reliability, cost efficiency, and certification credibility.

sections.title: St. Galler Business Model Patterns
sections.content:
Two St. Galler patterns apply to this market entry:

| # | Pattern | Fit | Rationale |
|---|---------|-----|-----------|
| 33 | **Lock-In** | PRIMARY | EN 81 type test qualification creates switching costs. Once an OEM qualifies ZOLLERN as a rail supplier, re-qualification of a replacement takes 6-12 months. |
| 40 | **Solution Provider** | SECONDARY | Moving from L5 (profile) to L4 (rail kit: profile + fishplates + clips) increases customer dependency and share of wallet. |
| 29 | **Ingredient Branding** | ASPIRATIONAL | Long-term: "Powered by ZOLLERN guide rails" on elevator spec sheets. Requires brand building. |

sections.title: Osterwalder 9-Block Canvas
sections.content:
### Block 1: Customer Segments

| Segment | Size (DACH) | Decision Maker | Buying Behavior |
|---------|------------|---------------|----------------|
| Elevator OEMs (Tier 1) | 4 companies: Schindler, KONE, Otis, TK Elevator | Central procurement + regional engineering | Framework contracts, dual/triple sourcing, long qualification |
| Regional elevator builders (Tier 2) | ~50 companies in DACH | Owner/technical director | Price-sensitive, relationship-driven, faster qualification |
| Elevator modernization specialists | ~30 companies in DACH | Project manager | Project-by-project ordering, delivery speed critical |
| Independent installers | ~200 companies in DACH | Purchasing manager | Buy from distributors, not direct |

### Block 2: Value Propositions

| Proposition | Target Segment | Differentiation |
|------------|---------------|----------------|
| 35% material cost savings via NNS rolling | All segments | Unique — no competitor markets this |
| In-house induction hardening (no subcontracting) | Tier 1 OEMs | Quality control + shorter lead time |
| DACH proximity — 2-5 day delivery | Tier 2 + modernization | vs 10-20 days from Italy/Switzerland |
| Custom profile development capability | Tier 1 OEMs | Can design non-standard rail profiles for special applications |
| EN 10204 Type 3.1 material certificates | All segments | Standard but not always offered by Asian imports |

### Block 3: Channels

| Channel | Role | Cost to Serve | Coverage |
|---------|------|-------------|----------|
| Direct technical sales (2-3 KAMs) | Tier 1 OEM accounts | HIGH | Schindler, KONE, TK Elevator, Otis |
| Regional sales manager | Tier 2 builders | MEDIUM | DACH territory |
| Steel distributors (Kloeckner, Thyssenkrupp Materials) | Independent installers | LOW | Indirect, volume aggregation |
| Trade fairs (interlift, Lift Expo) | Brand awareness, lead generation | MEDIUM | Biennial events |

### Block 4: Customer Relationships

| Type | Segment | Mechanism |
|------|---------|-----------|
| Dedicated key account management | Tier 1 OEMs | Named KAM, quarterly business reviews, joint product development |
| Technical support | Tier 2 builders | Application engineering for profile selection, tolerance advice |
| Transactional | Distributors | Standard catalog, web portal, EDI ordering |

### Block 5: Revenue Streams

| Stream | Pricing Model | Contribution (Year 3 est.) |
|--------|-------------|--------------------------|
| T89/B guide rail profiles (per meter / per ton) | Cost-plus with steel surcharge index | 70% |
| Induction hardening (value-add service) | Per-meter surcharge | 15% |
| Custom profile development (NRE) | One-time tooling fee | 5% |
| Cut-to-length + joint face machining | Per-piece processing fee | 10% |

### Block 6: Key Resources

| Resource | Type | Status |
|----------|------|--------|
| Hot rolling mill (Laucherthal) | Physical | EXISTING — idle capacity |
| Induction hardening line | Physical | EXISTING |
| T89/B roll tooling | Physical | INVEST — EUR 15-20K |
| EN 81 type test certification | Intangible | INVEST — EUR 30-50K |
| Technical sales personnel (elevator domain) | Human | HIRE — 1-2 KAMs with elevator industry experience |
| ISO 7465 + EN 81 expertise | Intellectual | BUILD — training + consultant engagement |

### Block 7: Key Activities

| Activity | Description | Frequency |
|----------|-------------|-----------|
| Profile rolling + hardening + cutting | Core production | Continuous (batch production) |
| Quality assurance (dimensional, hardness, straightness) | In-process + final inspection | Every batch |
| Customer qualification audits | OEM supplier audits, process capability studies | 2-4 per year |
| Roll/die maintenance | Preventive maintenance on tooling | Monthly |
| Market intelligence | Monitor competitor pricing, new elevator installations | Quarterly |

### Block 8: Key Partnerships

| Partner | Role | Strategic Value |
|---------|------|----------------|
| Steel mills (ArcelorMittal, Salzgitter) | Billet supply | Secured raw material supply, volume discounts |
| TUeV Sued / notified body | EN 81 type testing, periodic surveillance | Market access certification |
| Induction hardening equipment OEM (EFD Induction, SMS Elotherm) | Equipment maintenance, process optimization | Process reliability |
| Freight / logistics partner | Steel transport DACH region | Delivery reliability (2-5 day target) |

### Block 9: Cost Structure

| Cost Element | Share (est.) | Type | Behavior |
|-------------|-------------|------|----------|
| Raw material (steel billets) | 42% | Variable | Indexed to LME/steel prices |
| Direct labor (rolling, hardening, cutting) | 18% | Semi-fixed | IG Metall tariff, shift-dependent |
| Energy (gas + electricity for rolling/hardening) | 12% | Variable | Indexed to energy prices |
| Tooling amortization | 4% | Fixed (per profile) | Spread over production volume |
| Quality / testing | 5% | Semi-fixed | Per-batch and per-order |
| Sales & administration | 8% | Fixed | KAMs, order processing, logistics planning |
| Overhead (factory, depreciation) | 11% | Fixed | Allocated from existing facility |

sections.title: Financial Summary (Year 3 Base Case)
sections.content:
| Metric | Value | Calculation |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| Revenue | EUR 1.8M | 1,200 t x EUR 1,500/t |
| COGS | EUR 1.26M | 70% of revenue |
| Gross margin | EUR 540K | 30% gross margin |
| SGA | EUR 180K | 10% of revenue (allocated KAM + overhead) |
| EBIT | EUR 360K | 20% EBIT margin |
| Contribution to idle capacity absorption | 1,200 t / 7,000 t idle = 17% | Significant utilization improvement |
